beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, quick MLS scroll on Mercer Island.
beats.reasoning: Linda wakes early as always, low energy from yesterday (35/100) means she keeps it brief—coffee and work emails only, no power walk today given transit strike makes the day unpredictable.

beats.activity: Drive to Bellevue office, calls with clients en route.
beats.reasoning: Transit strike means Linda must drive; she budgets extra time and uses commute to network via car calls—typical for her workflow.

beats.activity: Team meeting, MLS updates, client prep at office.
beats.reasoning: Thursday morning routine: team sync and listing reviews before the day's open houses.

beats.activity: Drive to first open house in downtown Bellevue.
beats.reasoning: Short local drive within Bellevue; Linda leaves office to prep for first showing of the day.

beats.activity: Host open house in downtown Bellevue; greet buyers, network with agents.
beats.reasoning: Open houses are Linda's bread and butter—she loves the energy and face-to-face networking; mild weather suits foot traffic.

beats.activity: Drive to Medina waterfront property for second showing.
beats.reasoning: Cross-town drive from downtown Bellevue to Medina; transit strike adds 5 min buffer, but Linda drives everywhere anyway.

beats.activity: Show luxury Medina waterfront property; high-end showing to serious buyers.
beats.reasoning: This is Linda's signature beat—Medina waterfront is where the real money is; she's been highlighting this property across recent days and expects strong interest.

beats.activity: Lunch at Ascend restaurant; network with developer contact.
beats.reasoning: Linda's go-to upscale lunch spot where she builds relationships; timing works well after Medina showing, and she's likely to reconnect with Marcus or another developer contact.

beats.activity: Drive back to office; calls with clients about afternoon leads.
beats.reasoning: Return to base to check voicemails, emails, and prep for any late-day showings; transit strike ended at 4pm but doesn't affect Linda's car-based schedule.

beats.activity: Office work: follow-up emails, listing updates, client calls.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon admin and client management; low energy day (35/100 carryover) means Linda stays focused on desk work rather than a third showing.

beats.activity: Drive home to Mercer Island; make calls during commute.
beats.reasoning: End-of-day drive home; Linda uses commute time for final client touches before evening wind-down.

beats.activity: Dinner, emails, MLS review, early wind-down on Mercer Island.
beats.reasoning: Low energy carryover means Linda hydrates, eats, catches up on listings, but stays in and rests—no evening social plans; preparing for good sleep.

diary: Started the day with my usual ritual—coffee on Mercer Island while scrolling MLS listings. The transit strike meant everyone was driving today, so I left early and had the Bellevue office mostly to myself for our team meeting. The downtown open house was steady; nothing spectacular, but I networked with a few younger agents who reminded me why I still love this work. The Medina waterfront showing was the real highlight—serious money, serious buyers. You can feel it when someone's ready to commit. Grabbed lunch with a developer contact at Ascend; those conversations always lead somewhere. Spent the afternoon back at the office fielding calls and emails. By the time I drove home, the light was perfect over the water. Tired but satisfied.
thought_process: The strike disrupted typical patterns, so I frontloaded my schedule and stayed mobile—kept the car as my office. I prioritized the Medina showing because luxury inventory moves fast, and those buyers felt genuinely engaged. The open house was routine but necessary for brand visibility. Networking at lunch felt important; you never know which connection pays off in six months. I was honestly relieved the day stayed manageable despite the chaos. At 55, I'm past the grind-it-out phase, but days like this remind me I still have good energy for the work. The buyers I met felt real, not tire-kickers. That matters more than volume now.
world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
